proposed_prompt: I push open the door of The Broken Wheel and step inside, pausing to let my eyes adjust. I look around to assess who is inside and what kind of atmosphere the inn has.
rationale: Direct entry into the only lit building is the safest path to information. Pausing to observe before speaking preserves options.
expected_upside: Discover NPCs, learn why the village is deserted, establish contacts for future turns.
risk: Walking into an unknown situation. Could be an ambush, a frightened crowd, or hostile NPCs.

proposed_prompt: Before approaching the inn, I circle the village perimeter quietly, checking if any other buildings show signs of activity — movement behind shutters, sounds, footprints in the mud.
rationale: Spending a turn observing costs little and may surface information not visible from the entrance. Especially useful if the desertion has an active cause.
expected_upside: May reveal hidden NPCs, threats, or explain the desertion without committing to an NPC interaction.
risk: Delays information gathering; if the threat is immediate, circling the village may not be the best use of time.

proposed_prompt: I knock firmly on the inn door and call out: 'Traveler seeking lodging. Anyone inside?'
rationale: A vocal approach signals non-threatening intent and may elicit a faster NPC response than quietly entering.
expected_upside: NPCs may open up more readily if approached openly. Could establish rapport with innkeeper.
risk: Reveals presence loudly. If there is an active threat in the village, this draws attention.
